formatted_address: 6310 Laguna Bay Ct, Houston, TX 77041, USA
pitch_rationale: Area-weighted across 20 roof segments from Google Solar API (imageryQuality=HIGH): 35.21° → 8:12. Solar's underlying DSM model reports ~5° MAE in published benchmarks (Satellite Sunroof, NeurIPS 2024).
footprint_rationale: The orange bounding box spans approximately 380 px (wide) × 342 px (tall), equating to 80.7 ft × 72.6 ft = 5,859 sq ft bounding area. The subject is a large, complex multi-section hip roof with no visible gable ends (fully hipped). The pool in the upper-right of the bounding box and paved patio/driveway areas in the lower-left and lower portions are excluded from the roof footprint. A fill factor of ~0.68 was applied to account for these non-roof areas, yielding ~3,984 sq ft. Roof edges were traced section by section: numerous short ridge lines sit atop each hip section (~39 ft total); hip lines are abundant at every corner and section intersection (~144 ft); valleys appear at the re-entrant angles where sections join (~28 ft); rake edges are minimal given the fully hipped design (~12 ft); eave lines run along the full perimeter of all sections (~212 ft). Confidence is moderate (0.45) due to the complex multi-plane geometry, oblique view angle, and partial shadowing making individual edge identification challenging.

findings.description: A distinctly lighter tan/gray flat or low-slope roof section is visible in the center of the roof complex, clearly contrasting in both color and texture from the surrounding dark gray hip-roof shingle sections. This section spans approximately 15–20 ft and uses a visibly different roofing material (consistent with modified bitumen or TPO).
findings.location_description: Center of subject roof, between the northern and southern hip-roof volumes

condition.rationale: The subject roof at 6310 Laguna Bay Court is a large, complex multi-hip residential roof with dark gray/charcoal shingles across its primary slopes. At this resolution and image quality, the shingle field is largely uniform in color with no visible tarps, moss/biological growth, obvious missing material, or structural sag along the ridgelines. The one clearly notable feature is a lighter-colored flat or low-slope section centrally located between the two main hip-roof volumes — its tan/gray color and smooth texture are distinctly different from the surrounding dark shingle planes, indicating a different roofing material (likely a membrane system). This is not necessarily a defect, but flat/low-slope transitions are higher-risk areas for water intrusion and warrant a close in-person look at seams, drains, and membrane condition. No other actionable findings were identified at this resolution.

fence_reason: vision roof area 4789 differs from solar 4186 by 14.4% (>12%)
